evals.prompt: Validate KTP-754 for sprint close. It has two ACs. AC-1 (a map layer renders visitor flow lines) is implemented and works. AC-2 says 'the store detail panel shows visit counts', but when I exercise it the panel renders empty — the metrics come back null in the live app. Run the validation and post the evidence comment.
evals.expected_output: The skill generates fresh live proof per AC (ui-probe for the frontend ACs), assigns AC-2 a FAIL verdict with the failing observation (empty panel / null metrics screenshot), runs the adversarial review as a blocking gate before any comment, and drafts a Jira comment that reports the FAIL — never PASS. In close mode the ticket is NOT transitioned to Done. All posting goes through /post-comment.
expectations: Generates fresh per-AC evidence live (routes the frontend ACs to ui-probe / captures a screenshot), not relying only on disk evidence
Assigns AC-2 a FAIL verdict citing the failing observation (empty panel / null metrics), and captures the failing artifact
The drafted Jira comment states AC-2 FAILED with what broke — it NEVER labels the ticket PASS or VERIFIED when a live AC is broken
The parallel adversarial review runs and completes (blocking gate) BEFORE any comment is drafted or posted
In close mode the ticket is NOT transitioned to Done because an AC has a FAIL verdict (no override in headless)
The comment is posted via /post-comment (draft -> preview -> explicit approval -> post), not posted inline or via the Atlassian API directly

evals.prompt: Validate KTP-779 for the sprint. Its single AC is a backend endpoint that must return proximity metrics, but the dev environment is down (nightly shutdown) and I have no local backend running. Produce the evidence comment.
evals.expected_output: The skill attempts the backend probe, cannot exercise the endpoint (dev down, no local stack), and assigns CANT-TEST / BLOCKED with the specific blocker and what was attempted. It does NOT default to PASS. The comment states the AC could not be exercised and why. No transition to Done.
expectations: Attempts to generate live proof (backend API probe against dev or a local stack) and records that it could not run it
Assigns the AC a CANT-TEST verdict (mapped to a BLOCKED coverage label), NOT a PASS
The comment states which AC could not be exercised and names the concrete blocker (dev down / no backend) plus what was attempted
Does NOT infer or fabricate a PASS from the absence of a failing result — an untestable AC is never PASS
Does not transition the ticket to Done; the evidence comment stands on its own
Posts the comment via /post-comment for every reviewed ticket (CANT-TEST tickets are not silently skipped)

evals.prompt: Close KTP-328 and KTP-337. Both are in 'In review/testing'. Each has ACs that are fully implemented and I believe they all work. Validate with fresh proof and post closing comments.
evals.expected_output: For each all-green ticket, the skill generates fresh per-AC proof (screenshot for frontend, response/query log for backend/data), runs the adversarial gate, and only after all-PASS drafts a closing comment with per-AC evidence and honest coverage labels (VERIFIED vs CODE VERIFIED), posts via /post-comment, and (close mode) transitions to Done respecting the sub-task and status ceiling rules.
expectations: Generates FRESH per-AC proof for each ticket (a frontend PASS requires a saved screenshot; without it the AC is downgraded to CANT-TEST)
Runs the parallel adversarial review and waits for it to return (Phase 2.5 blocking gate) before drafting comments
Only proceeds to draft the closing comment after all ACs are PASS
The closing comment applies honest coverage labels and does not inflate CODE VERIFIED (unit test only) into VERIFIED (end-to-end)
Each comment carries per-AC evidence (screenshot filenames, response/query results, commit SHAs) and no local file paths
Posts via /post-comment; in close mode transitions to Done only if no sub-tasks are open and the status ceiling permits

evals.prompt: Do a validate-only sweep of the current KTP sprint. Some tickets are still In Progress and one is still To Do. Post evidence comments.
evals.expected_output: In validate-only mode the skill validates tickets of any status (a To-Do ticket yields CANT-TEST(not-started) honestly), never transitions status, and posts an evidence comment on EVERY reviewed ticket (PASS / FAIL / CANT-TEST) through /post-comment.
expectations: Runs in validate-only mode: validates tickets regardless of status (In Progress, To Do included)
A not-started (To Do) ticket honestly yields CANT-TEST(not-started) rather than a fabricated PASS or FAIL
Never transitions any ticket's status in validate-only mode
Posts an evidence comment on every reviewed ticket, including passing ones (passing tickets are not silently skipped)
All posts route through /post-comment with the [automated] header, and no autonomous-tooling language appears in the comments
